text: James drew a sharp line between two kinds of interest that drive attention. Immediate interest — the pull of the exciting, the loud, the novel — requires no effort. Derived interest is different: it is attention paid to something not intrinsically compelling because the mind has learned to see it as connected to something it cares about deeply. A tax form is dull; a tax form connected to your family's financial security is not. James argued that the skill of voluntary attention is largely the skill of constructing derived interest — training the mind to build chains of association that make the worthy feel important enough to attend to.
question: According to James, what is 'derived interest'?

options.text: Attention drawn to objects that are inherently exciting or novel
Interest generated through past conditioning and habit
Attention paid to something by connecting it to a deeper concern
A secondary form of curiosity triggered by boredom
